channel.title: Events on Apache Software Foundation Events
channel.description: Recent content in Events on Apache Software Foundation Events

item.description: 2026 Events Community over Code Glasgow — October 11–14, Glasgow, UK

item.title: Apache Tomcat — Hackathon at Community over Code Glasgow 2026
item.description: Community over Code 2026 — Glasgow, UK, October 11–14 Register now | Event website | Hackathon overview Apache Tomcat — Hackathon Coordinator Name — markt@apache.org What We’re Working On We will be working on new features and enhancements to Apache Tomcat. The ideas list is being maintained on the project Wiki. We welcome additional suggestions on the dev@ list if you have an idea for something you’d like to work on.

item.title: Apache YourProject — Hackathon at Community over Code Glasgow 2026
item.description: Community over Code 2026 — Glasgow, UK, October 11–14 Register now | Event website | Hackathon overview Apache YourProject — Hackathon Coordinator Name — email@apache.org What We’re Working On We’re focusing on: Good first issues — small, well-scoped tasks suitable for new contributors: GitHub issues labeled “good first issue” Documentation improvements — help us improve our getting-started guides and API docs Bug fixes — a curated set of bugs that are approachable with mentoring support Resources Contribution guide Dev mailing list thread about the hackathon GitHub issues Slack / Discord / chat channel Dev environment setup Who’s Planning to Attend Name (role or area of expertise) Name (role or area of expertise) Getting Started Before the Event To make the most of hackathon time, we recommend:

item.title: Call For Volunteers, Community over Code 2026
item.description: Community over Code 2026 will be held in Glasgow, UK, October 11-14. Community over Code is an event run by the community, for the community. That’s you! We need your help to make it a success! We are looking for volunteers for a number of community engagement tasks. These roles include: Hackathon Coordination - Help organize and run the hackathon University Outreach - Connect with colleges and universities across multiple departments Meetup Outreach - Engage with local meetup groups Speaker Support - Provide coaching and feedback to speakers Design - Create visual materials for outreach and on-site If you are interested in volunteering for any of these jobs, please:

item.title: Community over Code Glasgow 2026
item.description: Community over Code 2026 — Glasgow, UK, October 11–14 Community over Code is the annual conference of the Apache Software Foundation — bringing together developers, community managers, and enthusiasts from across the ASF ecosystem. Get Involved Hackathon — Dedicated time and space for contributors to work together on real project tasks. 25 projects are already signed up. Call for Volunteers — Help make the event a success — hackathon coordination, university outreach, meetup engagement, speaker support, and design.

item.title: Community over Code Glasgow 2026 - Hackathon Outreach Calendar
item.description: Conference dates: October 11 - 14, 2026 - Glasgow, Scotland This page tracks the outreach tasks leading up to the hackathon, and follow-up tasks afterward. Each task needs a volunteer owner. Click “Volunteer for this task!” to send an email to the planning team. August 2026 MonTueWedThuFriSatSun 12 34Share hackathon page URL Post hackathon announceme5Post hackathon announcemeCross-post hackathon link6Write a 2-sentence hackat7Follow up with tentative 89 1011Review the sample projectProofread the main hackat12Add your project's logo/iWrite a "

item.title: Community over Code Glasgow 2026 - Volunteer Outreach Calendar
item.description: Conference dates: October 11-14, 2026 - Glasgow, Scotland This page tracks volunteer outreach tasks for Community over Code Glasgow - speaker support, local community engagement, attendee experience, and post-event follow-up. Each task needs a volunteer owner. NOTE: Speaker outreach tasks require coordination through Brian Proffitt and the track chairs. Speaker contact information is not publicly shared. Outreach resources: Glasgow Schools & Universities Glasgow Diversity & Inclusion Groups Glasgow Tech Meetups Glasgow guides (help us improve these!

item.title: Events
item.description: Current Events Community over Code Glasgow 2026 — October 11–14, Glasgow, UK Coordinate Your Community Event Here This site is available to all ASF project communities to organize and promote meetups, hackathons, sprints, release parties, or any community gathering. Whether you’re planning something local or virtual, we can help you get the word out. To get started: Read the How to Organize a Small Apache Event guide Copy one of our event page templates Submit a PR to this repo with your event page Send a note to dev@community.

item.title: Glasgow Bars & Pubs
item.description: A guide to bars, pubs, and nightlife near the Community over Code Glasgow venue (Hilton Glasgow, 1 William St, city centre). Glasgow has a legendary pub culture. Here are some highlights within walking distance of the conference venue. Good Beer Guide Pubs (CAMRA-recommended) Babbity Bowster What: Tucked-away pub-restaurant-hotel in Merchant City with a reputation for excellent beer, wine, and food. Beer garden. Where: 16-18 Blackfriars Street, Glasgow G1 1PE (map) Good for: Craft beer, quieter conversation, good food Classic Glasgow Pubs The Belle What: Mixed-clientele favourite with suits, students, and dogs by an open fire Where: Great Western Road, Glasgow (map) Good for: Cosy atmosphere, good pints, open fire in October Felson’s What: Pool tables, HD sports screens, burgers and pints Where: City centre, Glasgow (map) Good for: Groups, casual hangouts, watching football Hope & Vincent What: Pub/lounge bar with pool table, jukebox, and an eight-foot sports screen Where: City centre, Glasgow (map) Good for: Laid-back vibes, entertainment, group gatherings Cocktail Bars Tabac What: Inventive cocktails, bartenders who’ll tailor drinks to your taste Where: 10 Mitchell Lane, Glasgow G1 3NU (map) Good for: Evening drinks, small groups, interesting conversation The Alchemist What: Theatrical cocktails and international food Where: Unit 2, George House, George Square, Glasgow G2 1EH (map) Good for: Pre-dinner drinks, spectacle, good veggie food too (see food page) Live Music Venues with Good Bars Mono What: Vegan cafe/bar with record store and live music Where: 12 Kings Court, King Street, Glasgow G1 5RB (map) Good for: Indie/alternative music, vegan food, vinyl shopping (see food page) The Hug and Pint What: Vegan food upstairs, live music downstairs Where: 171 Great Western Road, Glasgow G4 9AW (map) Good for: Evening out combining food + music (see food page) Quirky/Unique Bars Tiki Bar & Kitsch Inn What: Tropical-themed bar with massive rum selection and cocktails in tiki mugs Where: 214 Bath Street, Glasgow G2 4HW (map) Good for: Fun group outings, rum lovers, Instagram photos Flight Club What: Social darts venue with vintage fairground decor, bottomless pizza, and cocktails Where: 32 North Frederick Street, Glasgow G1 2BS (map) Good for: Groups, team bonding, something different Whisky Bars The Pot Still What: Over 700 whiskies, a Glasgow institution since the 1880s Where: 154 Hope Street, Glasgow G2 2TH (map) Good for: Whisky lovers, real ale, traditional pub atmosphere Late-Night Options Many Glasgow pubs close at midnight on weeknights and 1am on weekends.

item.title: Glasgow Diversity & Inclusion Groups - Outreach Contacts
item.description: This page lists Glasgow-area diversity-in-tech groups and organizations. We’re inviting their members to attend and participate in Community over Code Glasgow (October 11-14, 2026) and the hackathon. Diversity-in-Tech Organizations Scotland Women in Technology (SWiT) Website: https://www.scotwomenintech.com/ About: Scotland-wide organization supporting women in technology. Runs annual awards, events, and networking. Relevance: Established community with engaged members who’d benefit from conference exposure. Ladies of Code (Glasgow) Website: https://www.meetup.com/ladies-of-code-glasgow/ About: Regular meetups celebrating and supporting women in the tech industry.

item.title: Glasgow Schools & Universities - Outreach Contacts
item.description: This page lists Glasgow-area educational institutions with computing/technology programmes. We’re inviting students and faculty to attend and participate in the hackathon at Community over Code Glasgow (October 11-14, 2026). If you’re volunteering for student outreach, use this list as your starting point. Offer free or discounted student passes and highlight the hackathon as a hands-on learning opportunity. Universities University of Glasgow - School of Computing Science Website: https://www.gla.ac.uk/schools/computing/ About: Ranked 6th in the UK for Computer Science (Times/Sunday Times 2025).

item.title: Glasgow Tech Meetups - Outreach Contacts
item.description: This page lists Glasgow-area tech meetups and developer communities. We’re inviting their members to attend Community over Code Glasgow (October 11-14, 2026) and participate in the hackathon. Active Glasgow Tech Meetups Python Glasgow Website: https://www.meetup.com/python-glasgow/ Schedule: 1st Wednesday of every month About: Monthly meetup for sharing perspectives and insights about Python. Hosts speakers from across the city. Venue: The Gamer Club Relevance: Python is central to many Apache projects. High overlap with potential hackathon participants.

item.title: Glasgow Tourist Highlights
item.description: Attending Community over Code Glasgow? Here are the top things to see and do in Glasgow beyond the conference. Most are free and within easy reach of the venue. Top Free Attractions Kelvingrove Art Gallery and Museum What: World-class art and natural history museum in a stunning red sandstone building. 22 galleries, everything from Dali to dinosaurs. Where: Argyle Street, Glasgow G3 8AG (map) Cost: Free Time needed: 2-3 hours Glasgow Cathedral What: Medieval cathedral dating to the 12th century - one of the few Scottish medieval churches to survive the Reformation intact Where: Castle Street, Glasgow G4 0QZ (map) Cost: Free Time needed: 45 min - 1 hour Glasgow Necropolis What: Atmospheric Victorian cemetery on a hill behind the Cathedral.

item.title: Glasgow Vegetarian & Vegan Restaurants
item.description: A guide to vegetarian and vegan-friendly dining near the Community over Code Glasgow venue (Hilton Glasgow, 1 William St, city centre). Glasgow was named Scotland’s most veg-friendly city, and you’ll find excellent plant-based options all within walking distance of the venue. Fully Vegan Restaurants The Glasvegan What: Plant-based fast food - burgers, hot dogs, loaded fries Where: 50 St Enoch Square, Glasgow G1 4DH (map) Good for: Quick, filling, affordable Mono What: Vegan cafe/bar in Merchant City with an in-house record store and live music Where: 12 Kings Court, King Street, Glasgow G1 5RB (map) Good for: Casual meals, evening hangouts, salads, pizzas, sandwiches The Hug and Pint What: Vegan food above a live music venue Where: 171 Great Western Road, Glasgow G4 9AW (map) Good for: Evening meals before/after a gig, creative Asian-inspired dishes Rose & Grants What: Fully vegan cafe, open 7 days for breakfast and lunch Where: 27 Trongate, Glasgow G1 5EZ (map) Good for: Breakfast/brunch.

item.title: Hackathon at Community over Code Glasgow 2026
item.description: Community over Code 2026 — Glasgow, UK, October 11–14 Community over Code 2026 — Glasgow, UK, October 11–14 Hackathon Hackathon We’re bringing back the hackathon! Community over Code Glasgow will include dedicated time and space for contributors — new and experienced — to sit together and work on real tasks: fixing bugs, writing docs, tackling issues that need focused attention, and onboarding new contributors. How it works Each participating project provides:
